question: What tables are there and how are they connected? Give me a short summary.
Main user details/filters/identifiers/classifications
sql:
-- main user filters
  SELECT
      dc.RealCID AS CID, -- primary user ID, sometimes referred to as CID or RealCID in other tables
      dc.GCID, -- secondary user ID
      DATE_FORMAT(dc.RegisteredReal, 'yyyy-MM-dd') AS RegisteredDate,
      dc.FirstDepositAmount, -- the amount in dollars of the users first deposit (a key KPI of the business)
      DATE_FORMAT(dc.FirstDepositDate, 'yyyy-MM-dd') AS FirstDepositDate, -- the date of the users first deposit
      dc.FTDPlatformID, --The ID relating to the area of the platform the user first deposited to: 4 = ISA/Moneyfarm, 3 = IBAN, 2 = Options, 1 = Trading Platform
      at.Name as FTDPlatformName, -- The name of the area of the platform the user first deposited to
      dc.CountryID, -- -- The ID relating to the Country of a user, can then be used to attain their Region too from the lookup table
      dc1.Name as Country, -- Primary CountryName field of a user
      dc1.MarketingRegionManualName as Region, -- primary Region field of a user
      dc.AffiliateID, -- the ID of the affiliate partner that referred/directed the user to signup
      dc.RegulationID, -- current financial regulatory body ID of the user
      dr.Name as RegulationName, -- such as FCA or CySec 
      dc.PlayerLevelID, -- the ID relative the the users "club" level
      pl.Name as ClubStatus, --the current club status of a user (can change depending amount the user deposits/equity level of the user)
      dc.SubChannelID, -- the ID for the subchannel of acquisition
      dc3.Channel, --the name of the Channel of acquisition
      dc3.SubChannel, -- the name of the SubCannel of acquisition (in hierarchical order of Channel > SubChannel)   
      dc.VerificationLevelID, -- the current onboarding verification level of the user depending on KYC completion and other verification tasks (V0 being the lowest level of verified)
      dc.PlayerStatusID, -- the ID associated with the general current status (for example blocked) of the user
      ps.Name as PlayerStatus, -- the name associated with the general current status (for example blocked) of the user
      dc.AccountManagerID, --the ID of the current Account Manager of this user
      am.FirstName as AccountManagerFirstName,
      am.LastName as AccountManagerLastName, -- can be concatenated with the First name to get the user's current Account Manager's Full Name
      dc.GuruStatusID, -- the ID relating to the current Guru level of the user (predominantly in reference to Popular Investors or CopyTraders, this level changes the more "Copiers" the user has) 
      gs.GuruStatusName, -- the name relating to the current Guru level of the user (predominantly in reference to Popular Investors or CopyTraders, this level changes the more "Copiers" the user has). You can then see their number of copiers via the dc.NumOfCopiers field.
      dc.IsValidCustomer, -- a boolean value of whether the user is a valid customer or not, should always IsValidCustomer = 1 when analysing regular clients unless otherwise stated
      dc.Gender, -- user's Gender (F, M, U or NULL)
      dc.BirthDate
  FROM main.pii_data.gold_sql_dp_prod_we_dwh_dbo_dim_customer dc
  JOIN main.dwh.gold_sql_dp_prod_we_dwh_dbo_dim_country dc1 ON dc.CountryID = dc1.CountryID
  JOIN main.dwh.gold_sql_dp_prod_we_dwh_dbo_dim_channel dc3 ON dc.SubChannelID = dc3.SubChannelID
  JOIN main.dwh.gold_sql_dp_prod_we_dwh_dbo_dim_gurustatus gs ON gs.GuruStatusID = dc.GuruStatusID
  JOIN main.dwh.gold_sql_dp_prod_we_dwh_dbo_dim_playerlevel pl ON pl.PlayerLevelID = dc.PlayerLevelID
  JOIN main.dwh.gold_sql_dp_prod_we_dwh_dbo_dim_playerstatus ps ON ps.PlayerStatusID = dc.PlayerStatusID
  JOIN main.dwh.gold_sql_dp_prod_we_dwh_dbo_dim_manager am ON am.ManagerID = dc.AccountManagerID
  JOIN main.dwh.gold_sql_dp_prod_we_dwh_dbo_dim_regulation dr ON dr.ID = dc.RegulationID
  JOIN main.pii_data.gold_sql_dp_prod_we_dwh_dbo_dim_affiliate da ON da.AffiliateID = dc.AffiliateID
  JOIN main.bi_db.bronze_moneybusdb_dictionary_accounttypes at ON at.ID = dc.FTDPlatformID
  WHERE IsValidCustomer = 1 -- a boolean value of whether the user is a valid customer or not, should always IsValidCustomer = 1 when analysing regular clients unless otherwise stated.
usage_guidance: Use this as a base/example of all frequently used and key user details/filters/identifiers/classifications. This should help identify relevant joins and relationships when asked to return data for a specific set of users with certain characteristics or details. This can also identify when a user Registered, First Deposited and how much their First Deposit was - this in turn can help answer questions like "how many user Registered on a certain date?" or "how many users First Deposited on a certain date?"

instruction: ensure to filter/join also on bronze_sub_accounts_accounts by providerName = 'Moneyfarm' and filter/join also on EventPayloadRowData_ProviderName = 'Moneyfarm' when joining these two tables. This ensures bronze_sub_accounts_accounts is "one" in the "one to many" relationship.
